findings.issue: API entrypoint generates and overwrites JWT keypair on every startup (php bin/console lexik:jwt:generate-keypair --overwrite --no-interaction in docker-entrypoint.sh) instead of using externally managed production keys.
API entrypoint runs migrations and role seeding during web container startup (doctrine:migrations:migrate and app:seed-roles in docker-entrypoint.sh) rather than as explicit deploy/ops steps.
Database readiness/migration logic is gated by searching .env for DATABASE_URL (grep -q ^DATABASE_URL= .env) and can be skipped when env is injected only at runtime.
Compose defaults include weak fallback secrets (POSTGRES_PASSWORD and Mercure JWT secret defaults in compose.yaml) that can be accidentally used in production.
Production compose uses plain environment substitution for sensitive values (APP_SECRET, DB password, Mercure key in compose.prod.yaml) without Docker secrets integration.
Production frontend service has no healthcheck in compose configuration, reducing readiness visibility compared to API/database/redis checks.
